text: The lungs are clear without focal consolidation.
No pleural effusion or pneumothorax is identified.
The cardiomediastinal silhouette is within normal limits.
There is cardiomegaly with pulmonary vascular congestion.
Bilateral lower lobe atelectasis is present.
No acute osseous abnormalities are identified.
There is a small right pleural effusion.
The aortic knob is calcified.
Pulmonary vasculature is unremarkable.
There is patchy opacity in the right lower lobe.
The trachea is midline.
No pneumothorax is seen.
The diaphragms are well-defined.
Heart size is mildly enlarged.
No free air below the right hemidiaphragm.
The mediastinal and hilar contours are unremarkable.
Mild pulmonary edema is present.
No focal airspace disease is identified.
There is a right-sided pleural effusion.
Bony structures are intact.
